prompt:
You are a B2B prospecting analyst for a web agency. Analyze this locksmith's services page.

HOT LEAD signals:
- All services dumped on one page with no structure
- No individual service pages (bad for SEO)
- Missing service descriptions — just a bullet list
- No distinction between emergency and scheduled work
- No photos of actual work

COLD LEAD signals:
- Individual pages per service type (residential, commercial, auto, emergency)
- Detailed descriptions of what each service involves
- Clear emergency vs scheduled distinction
- Service-specific CTAs
- Before/after photos
- FAQ per service

Return each record as:
{
  "url": "<page url>",
  "pageName": "<short name>",
  "summary": "<1-2 sentence overview of services presentation>",
  "opportunityScore": "<1-10, higher = worse services page = better lead>",
  "serviceStructure": "<individual-pages|single-page|minimal|none>",
  "servicesListed": ["<service types found>"],
  "hasEmergencyDistinction": true/false,
  "hasServiceDescriptions": true/false,
  "topOpportunity": "<what a web agency could sell here>",
  "notes": "<one sentence prospecting assessment>"
}

---
You are analyzing web pages for the category described above.
For each page provided below, extract one record.
Follow the JSON schema from the category description exactly.

## Required fields (always include)
- "url": the page URL
- "pageName": short human-readable page name
- "summary": 1-2 sentence plain-language overview

## Field naming conventions (follow strictly)
- Scores: name ends with "Score", value is a number 1-10 (e.g. "conversionScore": 7)
- Booleans: name starts with "has"/"is" or ends with "Visible" (e.g. "pricingVisible": true)
- Short text: "notes", "description" — 1-2 sentences max
- Tags/lists: arrays of short strings (e.g. "classStyles": ["Hatha", "Vinyasa"])
- Plain strings: everything else (e.g. "price": "€15 drop-in")

Return a JSON object: { "records": [<one record per page>] }.
No markdown, no code fences.
response:
{
  "records": [
    {
      "url": "https://www.londonlocksmith.co.uk/locked-out.html",
      "pageName": "Locked Out Service Page",
      "summary": "The locked-out page is dominated by a contact enquiry form and an extensive navigation menu listing lock products, but contains no actual service description, no explanation of the lockout process, and no distinction between emergency and scheduled work. The page essentially functions as a contact form with no persuasive or informational content.",
      "opportunityScore": 8,
      "serviceStructure": "minimal",
      "servicesListed": ["locked out", "nightlatches", "mortice locks", "window locks", "locking bolts", "lock changing", "safes", "CCTV", "burglar bars", "digital locks", "CISA locks", "services for landlords", "professional witness", "forensic locksmith", "bailiff and property repossession"],
      "hasEmergencyDistinction": false,
      "hasServiceDescriptions": false,
      "topOpportunity": "Build a dedicated emergency lockout landing page with clear response time promise, process explanation, trust signals, and a prominent emergency CTA separate from scheduled bookings",
      "notes": "This is a high-value search-intent page (locked out) with virtually no content — a prime candidate for a conversion-optimised emergency service page rebuild."
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.londonlocksmith.co.uk/lock-changing/index.html",
      "pageName": "Lock Changing Service Page",
      "summary": "The lock changing page appears to contain only a contact form and navigation menu with no visible body content describing the lock changing service itself, its process, pricing guidance, or use cases. It mirrors the locked-out page in its lack of substantive service content.",
      "opportunityScore": 8,
      "serviceStructure": "individual-pages",
      "servicesListed": ["lock changing", "nightlatches", "mortice locks", "window locks", "locking bolts", "safes", "CCTV", "burglar bars", "digital locks", "CISA locks", "services for landlords", "professional witness", "forensic locksmith", "bailiff and property repossession"],
      "hasEmergencyDistinction": false,
      "hasServiceDescriptions": false,
      "topOpportunity": "Develop fully written service pages per lock type with descriptions of the process, when lock changing is needed, brand options, and scenario-specific CTAs (e.g. after a break-in, moving home, lost keys)",
      "notes": "Individual service URLs exist which is a good structural foundation, but all pages lack actual content — strong opportunity to sell content-led SEO page rewrites."
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.londonlocksmith.co.uk/book-a-locksmith/",
      "pageName": "Book a Locksmith Booking Form",
      "summary": "This page is a detailed job booking form capturing visit preferences, full contact details, address, parking, congestion/ULEZ charges, and payment method — it is functional and thorough but serves purely as an operational intake form with no service marketing content. There is no service description, no trust-building elements, and no contextual information to reassure a prospective customer.",
      "opportunityScore": 5,
      "serviceStructure": "none",
      "servicesListed": [],
      "hasEmergencyDistinction": false,
      "hasServiceDescriptions": false,
      "topOpportunity": "Redesign the booking flow with a modern multi-step UX, add trust signals (reviews, accreditations) near the form, and introduce a service-selection step that cross-sells relevant add-ons",
      "notes": "The booking form is operationally detailed but has no conversion copy or reassurance elements — a UX and trust-optimisation project would be a natural sell alongside any service page work."
    }
  ]
}
